name: Resolution-Cost Gradient
oneSentenceClaim: Missing evidence should cause harsher downgrades when resolving it requires high-cost, unavailable, or unstable execution.
whyExistingPrinciplesInsufficient: Existing concepts identify risk sources but do not predict dominance among falsifier access, resolution cost, executable specificity, and controls.
mechanism: decision severity scales with public resolution cost and expected replay burden
nonExamples: private data or unavailable protocol clarification
falsificationTests: rival predicts observation with fewer caveats
